# task\-audit\-shared\-ledger\-resolution — Make ledger resolution explicit and support shared worktree coordination
status: done  priority: 1  scope: scope-git
readiness: not_eligible  reason: status_done
commits: 143d4b1

Stop treating an unrelated directory as an empty Waystation project and add an explicit shared\-ledger operating mode for agents in separate Git worktrees\. Resolve the ledger by explicit root, environment override, then upward discovery\. Keep ledger storage/locking separate from the invoking agent's Git branch and worktree context so a shared claim records the real caller\.

## Acceptance
- Every non\-init operation returns a ledger\_not\_found diagnostic when no \.waystation directory can be resolved\.
- Ledger selection precedence is explicit \-\-root, WAYSTATION\_ROOT, then upward discovery from the caller directory\.
- CLI, MCP, and dashboard startup paths use the same resolver and disclose the selected ledger root\.
- A claim written from worktree B into a ledger rooted elsewhere records worktree B and its branch while locking and writing only the selected ledger\.
- A concurrent two\-worktree test targeting one ledger proves exactly one same\-task claim succeeds\.
- ADR\-0003 and user documentation distinguish checkout\-local default behavior from opt\-in shared\-ledger coordination\.
